info.name: Transsmart APIv2
info.description:
This Postman package serves as a guideline for integrating any type of logistic business application with Transsmart via the API interface, version 2.
The package is not meant to describe how Transsmart operates in the logistics process-chain, but rather assumes the reader to be familiar with the business processes involved on the side of the client or Transsmart.

The complete reference of all requests, responses, data types, enumerators, etc can be found here : https://devdocs.transsmart.com.
That resource also contains a few QuickGuides that can help you get up and running soon.

item.name: 0. Introduction
item.description:
Before you are able to actively use this Postman package, you should have received some credentials from your account manager, consultant or our support department.
Basically the credentials are threefold:
* username
* password
* account code

If you don't have these credentials, please check with your business colleagues or get in contact with our support department via support@transsmart.com.

Transsmart has two environments publicly available: user acceptance and production.
Below are the corresponding base urls.
* user acceptance : https://accept-api.transsmart.com
* production : https://api.transsmart.com

When using date or times, they are always considered within the UTC+1 timezone. If you are working from a different timezone, please make sure to convert the date/time before using it in the call to the endpoint.

item.description:
https://devdocs.transsmart.com/#_authenticating

APIv2 uses token based authentication. All tokens are valid for 24 hours. When using expired tokens, the APIv2 will respond with a 401 - Unauthorized.
`JWT expired at 2018-12-05T01:19:17Z. Current time: 2019-01-27T21:15:43Z, a difference of 4650986872 milliseconds. Allowed clock skew: 0 milliseconds.`

Requesting a token is easy. Just send a basic authentication GET request to the environment base url + "/login", using the username and password provided to you.
The response will contain a JWT token.

Once you have retrieved the token, you can use it with every subsequent call to the APIv2.
Don't have any means of adding the JWT from code directly? In that case just add a http header "Authorization" with value "Bearer "+token.

request.description:
https://devdocs.transsmart.com/#_shipment_booking_and_printing

Documents are generated and queued for printing by SmartPrint.
The print configuration of the integration user is used to select the SmartPrint and the printers where the print jobs needs to be forwarded to.
The response will not contain the actual documents, only the confirmation that the documents were queued successfully.

request.description:
https://devdocs.transsmart.com/#_shipment_booking_and_printing

Documents are generated and queued for printing by SmartPrint.
In this case the call parameters indicate what SmartPrint connection and what printers to use for queue the print job.
The response will not contain the actual documents, only the confirmation that the documents were queued successfully.

query.description: Wether or not to return the documents as part of the response.

item.description:
https://devdocs.transsmart.com/#_shipment_booking_only

With BOOK as {action} path parameter the shipment is booked only. In this case the request parameter rawJob is ignored.

The body is a json of the shipment object, wrapped in a json array. This will allow for multiple shipments to be booked in a single POST operation.

When using PRINT as {action} path parameter the system will start by booking the shipment, but the documents for this shipment are created on the fly as well.
Depending on the value of the rawJob querystring parameter the documents are either returned as part of the response or are queued to SmartPrint.

item.description:
https://devdocs.transsmart.com/#_shipment_manifesting

By using this call the client can trigger the manifesting of shipments.
Please consult with the Transsmart consultant or project manager on the need for this call, because in many cases manifesting will be triggered preferably by cutoff times on the Transsmart platform.

https://devdocs.transsmart.com/#_shipment_booking

The template URL for booking shipments is this : {base_url}/v2/shipments/{account}/{action}

There are 2 generic use cases for booking shipments, depending on the {action} path parameter:
1. BOOK : simply book a shipment, the response contains a copy of the shipment data as it is available in Transsmart after processing the POST request
2. PRINT : first book a shipment, secondly also generate the documents to be printed

When the action is PRINT, the rawJob request parameter determines wether or not to return the documents as part of the response or to place the corresponding print jobs in the queue for SmartPrint (i.e. for the Transsmart print solution).
* rawJob=true : the response contains a list of all generated documents in base64 encoded format
* rawJob=false : the system will generate the documents and queue them for printing, the response contains simply a confirmation of success

All three combinations (BOOK, PRINT rawJob=true and PRINT rawJob=false) can take the same input, but the response will be distinctly different.

**Important**
Transsmart offers a lot of options when it comes to booking and printing shipments. Therefor, regardless of your technical capabilities of calling the APIv2, you should also consult the Transmart project manager or consultant on the best implemenation option for your specific case. The provided examples only show a generic example.

request.description:
https://devdocs.transsmart.com/#_retrieve_status_for_shipments_based_on_query_parameters_without_detailed_information

The endpoint takes a few parameters into consideration to filter the results.

The maxResults parameter can be used to reduce the number of matching shipment status updates. This helps in query performance and in response generation.
Sidenote : any status update on package level will mean that the complete shipment (including the other packages) is returned as part of the response.

When setting the isIncremental parameter to true, the system will flag the retrieved status updates. They will not be included in any subsequent calls to the endpoint. That way it is possible to continue doing batch retrieval of updates with the same set of parameters until no more results are returned. An empty response is an indication that all status updates for that specific filter have been retrieved.

item.description:
https://devdocs.transsmart.com/#_shipment_status_tracking

Endpoint for getting status information for a single shipment or for multiple shipments based on a query.

There are 2 querystring parameters that can be used in either case:
1. isDetailed : boolean true/false, wether to included all details of the shipment or only a limited number of fields
2. currentStatusOnly : boolean true/false, effective only when isDetailed=true, wether to restrict the status information only to the last known status on shipment and collo level or to return all status events in chronological order

Note: we prefer using Streamed Statuses to push status updates to your system. Please check https://devdocs.transsmart.com/#_5_2_status_stream_pilot_phase for more information

item.description:
https://devdocs.transsmart.com/#_pick_up_locations_determination

This endpoint returns the locations the closest to the reference address. The actual data is fetched in real-time from the providers' backend.
If no provider (carrier) is selected, the system will try to forward the request to all providers configured for your account and supporting location selection.

If multiple or all configured providers are requested, the API executes the requests simultaneously to the carriers. This multithreaded request will wait for all carriers to have returned their info or to have timed out.

Since the fetching of locations is done in real-time and carrier response times might be slightly higher than expected, latency can occur. As the requests are multithreaded and are returned on success or timeout (10 seconds) of the requested carriers, it can occur that a noticeable latency of a single carrier will influence the result of the multiple requested carriers.
Therefor, if possible, we encourage you to implement the execution of multiple requests per single carrier. This will allow you to dynamicly build up the check-out page as results flow in.

Besides the actual data, the response also contains the carrier logo and a datamarker icon as base64 encoded string.

This resource reports the number of shipments and the corresponding shipment types that the account booked during the selected time period.
Shipment type can either be parcel or pallet.
The 2 consolidation parameters (consolidateAccounts and consolidateCarriers) can be used to get more insight on sub-account and carrier level.

The max allowed report period is 1 year, i.e. the time between startDate and endDate.
